psychology_missing_courses.title: Statistical Methods in Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Application of statistical methods to psychological research including descriptive statistics, inferential statistics, and data analysis techniques. Students learn to analyze psychological data and interpret statistical results using statistical software.
learning_objectives: Apply descriptive and inferential statistical methods to psychological data
Use statistical software for data analysis and interpretation
Understand probability theory and hypothesis testing
Conduct appropriate statistical tests for different research designs
Interpret and communicate statistical results effectively
covered_materials: Effect size and statistical power analysis

psychology_missing_courses.title: Cognitive Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Study of mental processes including perception, attention, memory, language, and problem-solving. Students explore cognitive theories and research findings while understanding how humans process information and make decisions.
learning_objectives: Understand major theories of cognitive processes
Analyze research methods used in cognitive psychology
Explain mechanisms of attention, memory, and perception
Evaluate language processing and comprehension theories
Apply cognitive principles to real-world problems

psychology_missing_courses.title: Social Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Examination of how social factors influence individual behavior, attitudes, and cognition. Students learn about social influence, group dynamics, interpersonal relationships, and social cognition processes.
learning_objectives: Understand social influence processes and conformity
Analyze attitude formation and attitude change
Explain group dynamics and intergroup relations
Evaluate theories of attraction and interpersonal relationships
Apply social psychological principles to contemporary issues

psychology_missing_courses.title: Abnormal Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Study of psychological disorders including classification, symptoms, causes, and treatment approaches. Students learn about various mental health conditions while understanding the complexity of abnormal behavior and its assessment.
learning_objectives: Understand classification systems for psychological disorders
Analyze causes and risk factors for mental health conditions
Evaluate diagnostic criteria and assessment methods
Examine treatment approaches and therapeutic interventions
Understand cultural and ethical considerations in mental health

psychology_missing_courses.title: Personality Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Comprehensive study of personality theories, assessment methods, and individual differences. Students explore major personality theories while learning about personality development, measurement, and applications in various contexts.
learning_objectives: Understand major theories of personality development
Evaluate personality assessment methods and tools
Analyze individual differences and personality traits
Examine personality stability and change across the lifespan
Apply personality psychology to practical settings
covered_materials: Trait theories and the Big Five model
Personality and health, relationships, and work

psychology_missing_courses.title: Biological Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Exploration of the biological foundations of behavior including brain structure, neurotransmitter systems, and the relationship between biology and psychological processes. Students learn about the nervous system and its role in behavior.
learning_objectives: Understand nervous system structure and function
Explain neurotransmitter systems and their effects
Analyze the biological basis of psychological disorders
Evaluate research methods in biological psychology
Apply biological perspectives to understanding behavior

psychology_missing_courses.title: Learning and Memory
psychology_missing_courses.description: Detailed study of learning processes and memory systems including classical conditioning, operant conditioning, and cognitive approaches to learning. Students explore how organisms acquire, store, and retrieve information.
learning_objectives: Understand principles of classical and operant conditioning
Analyze cognitive approaches to learning and memory
Explain memory systems and processes
Evaluate factors affecting learning and memory performance
Apply learning principles to educational and therapeutic settings
covered_materials: Observational learning and social learning theory
Working memory and long-term memory systems

psychology_missing_courses.title: Clinical Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Introduction to clinical psychology practice including assessment, diagnosis, and treatment of psychological disorders. Students learn about therapeutic approaches, ethical considerations, and the role of clinical psychologists.
learning_objectives: Understand the scope and practice of clinical psychology
Learn psychological assessment and diagnostic procedures
Evaluate major therapeutic approaches and interventions
Understand ethical and professional issues in clinical practice
Analyze case studies and treatment planning
covered_materials: History and scope of clinical psychology

psychology_missing_courses.title: Counseling Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Principles and practices of counseling psychology including counseling theories, techniques, and applications. Students learn about the counseling process, therapeutic relationship, and intervention strategies for various populations.
learning_objectives: Understand major counseling theories and approaches
Develop basic counseling skills and techniques
Learn about the therapeutic relationship and process
Understand multicultural counseling considerations
Apply counseling principles to specific populations and issues
covered_materials: Psychodynamic and systems approaches to counseling
Multicultural and diversity issues in counseling

psychology_missing_courses.title: Educational Psychology
psychology_missing_courses.description: Application of psychological principles to educational settings including learning theories, motivation, assessment, and classroom management. Students learn to apply psychological knowledge to improve educational outcomes.
learning_objectives: Apply learning theories to educational contexts
Understand motivation and its role in academic achievement
Evaluate educational assessment and measurement principles
Analyze classroom management and instructional strategies
Address individual differences and special educational needs
covered_materials: Motivation and goal setting in education
